mainEntity.name: What are Utah workers' comp benefits after falling from a ladder on the job in Murray?
mainEntity.text: What are Utah workers' comp benefits after falling from a ladder on the job in Murray?

acceptedAnswer.text: In Utah, workers' comp for ladder falls covers medical expenses, temporary total disability (TTD) at 66 2/3% of average weekly wage (up to $1,200/week max in 2026), permanent partial disability based on impairment rating, and vocational rehab if needed. Claims must be filed within 1 year.

suggestedAnswer.text: Utah's workers' compensation system provides coverage for ladder fall injuries including all reasonable medical care, wage replacement via TTD (2/3 AWW, max $1,200/week), and PPD awards using AMA Guides for impairment. If denied, appeal to the Utah Labor Commission within 20 days. Employers in Murray or Boise must carry insurance; uninsured claims go through the Uninsured Employer Fund. Consult local firms like Just Work Comp Law for jurisdiction-specific filing.
